# Exploration: `landing-foundation`

> Generated by `sdd-explore` on 2026-06-30 for change `landing-foundation` (project `landing-axilrent`).
> Read-only investigation. No code, dependencies, or config files were created or modified by this phase.

## Current State

**Project is greenfield.** Confirmed by `ls` of the working directory and `openspec/project.md` (generated by `sdd-init` on 2026-06-30):

- Only files present: `DESIGN.md`, `.atl/skill-registry.md`, `.git/`, `openspec/project.md` (this project's init mirror), plus a single init commit.
- No `package.json`, no `tsconfig.json`, no `app/`, no `src/`, no lockfile, no linter config.
- Git remote: `https://github.com/Cosifran/landing-axilrent.git` (one commit, `a89288b`).
- Pre-chosen tech conventions (from `sdd-init` engram observation + skill registry): Next.js 15 App Router, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind 4, Zustand 5, Zod 4, **pnpm**, **Vercel** deploy.
- Spanish-language landing for the Dominican Republic proptech market ("Axil rent"). UI copy and `<html lang>` are Spanish.

The design intent is fully captured in two artifacts:

1. **`DESIGN.md`** — a single "Axil Core" design system spec with 47 Material 3 color tokens, 6 typography tokens, 6 radius tokens, 7 spacing tokens, brand voice, layout/elevation rules, and component guidelines.
2. **`/tmp/opencode/axil-landing.html`** — the Stitch-generated full landing (hero, problem, solutions, functions, why-Axil, FAQ, contact, footer) using Tailwind v3 CDN with all design tokens already mapped to `tailwind.config.theme.extend`.

The Stitch HTML is the **structural reference for the design system mapping** but uses Tailwind v3 syntax. The proposal must translate those patterns to Tailwind v4 `@theme` block conventions.

## Affected Areas

Files this change will create (paths are proposed, not committed):

- `package.json`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `tsconfig.json`, `next.config.ts`, `next-env.d.ts` — scaffold output
- `postcss.config.mjs` — registers `@tailwindcss/postcss`
- `eslint.config.mjs` — `create-next-app --yes` default (flat config)
- `.gitignore` — scaffold output
- `app/globals.css` — `@import "tailwindcss"` + `@theme` block with all design tokens, plus custom `@utility` rules
- `app/layout.tsx` — root layout with `next/font` wiring, `lang="es"`, metadata, viewport
- `app/page.tsx` — placeholder ("coming soon" or empty section list)
- `app/_components/nav/nav.tsx` — Server Component shell
- `app/_components/nav/mobile-menu-toggle.tsx` — Client Component (menu state + scroll effect)
- `app/_components/nav/nav-link.tsx` (optional) — typed nav link primitive

Files this change will **not** touch (out of scope, follow-up changes): `app/hero/`, FAQ, contact form, footer, page-level sections.

## Design Token → Tailwind 4 `@theme` Mapping

All values come from `DESIGN.md`. CSS variable names follow Tailwind v4's namespace convention (`--color-*`, `--text-*`, `--font-*`, `--spacing-*`, `--radius-*`, `--shadow-*`, `--container-*`, `--font-weight-*`, `--tracking-*`).

### Colors — full `--color-*` namespace (49 tokens)

Tailwind v4 generates `bg-*`, `text-*`, `border-*`, `ring-*`, `fill-*`, `stroke-*` utilities from any `--color-{name}` defined in `@theme`. Naming the variables with Material 3 semantics (not numeric scale) is the **whole point of a design system** — it makes the Stitch HTML patterns like `bg-primary text-on-primary` work verbatim.

| DESIGN.md token | CSS variable | Value | Used by Nav? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `surface` | `--color-surface` | `#f7f9fb` | yes (mobile-menu bg ref via container-lowest) |
| `surface-dim` | `--color-surface-dim` | `#d8dadc` | no |
| `surface-bright` | `--color-surface-bright` | `#f7f9fb` | no |
| `surface-container-lowest` | `--color-surface-container-lowest` | `#ffffff` | **yes** (nav background) |
| `surface-container-low` | `--color-surface-container-low` | `#f2f4f6` | no (mobile-menu border-t ref) |
| `surface-container` | `--color-surface-container` | `#eceef0` | no |
| `surface-container-high` | `--color-surface-container-high` | `#e6e8ea` | no |
| `surface-container-highest` | `--color-surface-container-highest` | `#e0e3e5` | no |
| `on-surface` | `--color-on-surface` | `#191c1e` | **yes** (mobile-menu text) |
| `on-surface-variant` | `--color-on-surface-variant` | `#5b403f` | **yes** (desktop nav link color) |
| `inverse-surface` | `--color-inverse-surface` | `#2d3133` | no |
| `inverse-on-surface` | `--color-inverse-on-surface` | `#eff1f3` | no |
| `outline` | `--color-outline` | `#8f6f6e` | no |
| `outline-variant` | `--color-outline-variant` | `#e4bebc` | no |
| `surface-tint` | `--color-surface-tint` | `#bb152c` | no |
| `primary` | `--color-primary` | `#b7102a` | **yes** (brand, CTA, mobile toggle) |
| `on-primary` | `--color-on-primary` | `#ffffff` | **yes** (CTA text) |
| `primary-container` | `--color-primary-container` | `#db313f` | no (hover state for primary CTA) |
| `on-primary-container` | `--color-on-primary-container` | `#fffbff` | no |
| `inverse-primary` | `--color-inverse-primary` | `#ffb3b1` | no |
| `secondary` | `--color-secondary` | `#006d43` | no (status indicators) |
| `on-secondary` | `--color-on-secondary` | `#ffffff` | no |
| `secondary-container` | `--color-secondary-container` | `#75f8b3` | no |
| `on-secondary-container` | `--color-on-secondary-container` | `#007147` | no |
| `tertiary` | `--color-tertiary` | `#465d81` | no |
| `on-tertiary` | `--color-on-tertiary` | `#ffffff` | no |
| `tertiary-container` | `--color-tertiary-container` | `#5f759b` | no |
| `on-tertiary-container` | `--color-on-tertiary-container` | `#fefcff` | no |
| `error` | `--color-error` | `#ba1a1a` | no |
| `on-error` | `--color-on-error` | `#ffffff` | no |
| `error-container` | `--color-error-container` | `#ffdad6` | no |
| `on-error-container` | `--color-on-error-container` | `#93000a` | no |
| `primary-fixed` | `--color-primary-fixed` | `#ffdad8` | no |
| `primary-fixed-dim` | `--color-primary-fixed-dim` | `#ffb3b1` | no |
| `on-primary-fixed` | `--color-on-primary-fixed` | `#410007` | no |
| `on-primary-fixed-variant` | `--color-on-primary-fixed-variant` | `#92001c` | no |
| `secondary-fixed` | `--color-secondary-fixed` | `#78fbb6` | no |
| `secondary-fixed-dim` | `--color-secondary-fixed-dim` | `#59de9b` | no |
| `on-secondary-fixed` | `--color-on-secondary-fixed` | `#002111` | no |
| `on-secondary-fixed-variant` | `--color-on-secondary-fixed-variant` | `#005232` | no |
| `tertiary-fixed` | `--color-tertiary-fixed` | `#d5e3ff` | no |
| `tertiary-fixed-dim` | `--color-tertiary-fixed-dim` | `#b0c7f1` | no |
| `on-tertiary-fixed` | `--color-on-tertiary-fixed` | `#001b3c` | no |
| `on-tertiary-fixed-variant` | `--color-on-tertiary-fixed-variant` | `#30476a` | no |
| `background` | `--color-background` | `#f7f9fb` | no (used in body class) |
| `on-background` | `--color-on-background` | `#191c1e` | no |
| `surface-variant` | `--color-surface-variant` | `#e0e3e5` | no (mobile-menu border-t) |

**Classification — must be CSS variable vs. can stay as static value:**

- **All 49 should be CSS variables** in `@theme`. Rationale: every token in the table is referenced semantically by the Stitch HTML (e.g. `bg-primary-container`, `text-on-secondary-fixed-variant`). If we hardcode even one as a hex literal, we break the design system. The proposal should establish the full palette in one shot; do not pick-and-choose based on "does the Nav use it?" — that's how design systems rot.
- **Pure white** (`#ffffff`) is the value of `surface-container-lowest` AND a base Tailwind color (`white`). The nav background `bg-surface-container-lowest/80` (with 80% opacity) **must** be a CSS variable so the opacity modifier works. If we used `bg-white/80`, the 80% opacity would still work, but we'd lose semantic intent.

### Typography — `--font-*`, `--text-*`, `--leading-*`, `--tracking-*`, `--font-weight-*`

Tailwind v4 supports paired `line-height`, `letter-spacing`, and `font-weight` on a `--text-*` token using the `--text-*{suffix}` sub-namespace syntax. This is exactly the Stitch pattern, just relocated to CSS.

```css
@theme {
  --font-hanken: "Hanken Grotesk", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif;
  --font-inter: "Inter", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif;

  /* headline-xl: 48/56, weight 700, -0.02em */
  --text-headline-xl: 48px;
  --text-headline-xl--line-height: 56px;
  --text-headline-xl--letter-spacing: -0.02em;
  --text-headline-xl--font-weight: 700;

  /* headline-lg: 32/40, weight 600, -0.01em */
  --text-headline-lg: 32px;
  --text-headline-lg--line-height: 40px;
  --text-headline-lg--letter-spacing: -0.01em;
  --text-headline-lg--font-weight: 600;

  /* headline-lg-mobile: 24/32, weight 600, no tracking */
  --text-headline-lg-mobile: 24px;
  --text-headline-lg-mobile--line-height: 32px;
  --text-headline-lg-mobile--font-weight: 600;

  /* body-md: 16/24, weight 400 */
  --text-body-md: 16px;
  --text-body-md--line-height: 24px;
  --text-body-md--font-weight: 400;

  /* body-sm: 14/20, weight 400 */
  --text-body-sm: 14px;
  --text-body-sm--line-height: 20px;
  --text-body-sm--font-weight: 400;

  /* label-caps: 12/16, weight 600, 0.05em */
  --text-label-caps: 12px;
  --text-label-caps--line-height: 16px;
  --text-label-caps--letter-spacing: 0.05em;
  --text-label-caps--font-weight: 600;
}
```

This generates `text-headline-xl` (size+line-height+weight+tracking combined), `font-hanken`, `font-inter`, etc. — exactly the pattern Stitch uses (`font-headline-xl text-headline-xl`).

**Font loading**: `next/font/google` with Hanken Grotesk, Inter, and Material Symbols Outlined. Material Symbols is a variable font with **extra axes** (FILL, wght, GRAD, opsz) that must be requested via the `axes` option or the variation settings will not work.

```ts
// app/layout.tsx
import { Hanken_Grotesk, Inter, Material_Symbols_Outlined } from "next/font/google";

const hanken = Hanken_Grotesk({ subsets: ["latin"], display: "swap", variable: "--font-hanken-grotesk", weight: ["400", "600", "700", "800"] });
const inter = Inter({ subsets: ["latin"], display: "swap", variable: "--font-inter", weight: ["400", "500", "600"] });
const materialSymbols = Material_Symbols_Outlined({ subsets: ["latin"], display: "swap", variable: "--font-material-symbols", axes: ["FILL", "wght", "GRAD", "opsz"] });
```

**Important**: `next/font/google` exposes a CSS variable. The `@theme` `--font-hanken` token must reference that variable (not the Google Fonts name), so the build-time inlined font file is the actual source. Approach:

```css
@theme {
  --font-hanken: var(--font-hanken-grotesk), ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif;
  --font-inter: var(--font-inter), ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif;
}
```

### Spacing — `--spacing-*` and `--container-*`

The Stitch HTML uses semantic spacing names (`px-margin-mobile`, `gap-stack-md`, `max-w-[1200px]`). Mapping all of them preserves the designer's vocabulary.

```css
@theme {
  --spacing-base: 8px;
  --spacing-stack-sm: 12px;
  --spacing-stack-md: 24px;
  --spacing-stack-lg: 48px;
  --spacing-margin-mobile: 16px;
  --spacing-gutter: 24px;
  --container-max: 1200px;  /* generates max-w-max utility */
}
```

**Note on `container-max`**: Tailwind v4's `--container-*` namespace generates `max-w-*` utilities and also `@container` query variants. Using `max-w-max` is fine here. Alternative: use Tailwind's built-in `container` utility and customize it via `@utility container { max-width: 1200px; }` — cleaner for the "main page wrapper" pattern but loses the named-token feel. The proposal should pick one.

### Radius — `--radius-*`

```css
@theme {
  --radius-sm: 0.25rem;
  --radius-md: 0.75rem;
  --radius-lg: 1rem;
  --radius-xl: 1.5rem;
  --radius-full: 9999px;
}
```

**Conflict to flag for the user**: `DESIGN.md` says cards use **1.5rem (24px) radius** ("Rounded-XL"). The Stitch HTML actually uses `rounded-2xl` (1rem / 16px) for the "Why Axil?" cards and `rounded-lg` (0.5rem / 8px) for the pain-point cards. The proposed `@theme` includes both, but the proposal should ask the user which radius to use as the "default card" radius when building new components.

### Elevation (shadows) — `--shadow-*`

DESIGN.md calls these "ambient shadows" and "diffused floor shadows" — high blur, low opacity, with a tertiary color tint. Stitch HTML has two concrete values:

```css
@theme {
  --shadow-ambient: 0 10px 40px -10px rgba(70, 93, 129, 0.08);
  --shadow-ambient-hover: 0 20px 40px -10px rgba(70, 93, 129, 0.12);
}
```

Generates `shadow-ambient` and `shadow-ambient-hover` utilities. The nav uses plain `shadow-sm` (Tailwind built-in) plus the glass-blur background — different from card elevation.

### Glassmorphic (custom `@utility`)

The Stitch HTML defines `.glass-panel` (white/80 + backdrop-blur + thin border). This isn't a single-token concern — it's a multi-property effect that combines a color, a blur, and a border. Best modeled as a custom utility:

```css
@utility glass-panel {
  background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-surface-container-lowest) 80%, transparent);
  backdrop-filter: blur(12px);
  -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(12px);
  border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-surface-container-lowest) 30%, transparent);
}
```

**Note for proposal**: The nav is glassmorphic but simpler than `.glass-panel` — it's just `bg-surface-container-lowest/80 backdrop-blur-md`. Tailwind v4 supports opacity modifiers on any `bg-*` utility when the underlying color is defined in `@theme`, so `bg-surface-container-lowest/80` will work without a custom utility for the nav specifically. The full `glass-panel` utility is needed later (for the hero mock card), not in this change.

### Material Symbols — `font-variation-settings` utility

Stitch HTML has:

```css
.material-symbols-outlined { font-variation-settings: 'FILL' 0, 'wght' 400, 'GRAD' 0, 'opsz' 24; }
.material-symbols-outlined.filled { font-variation-settings: 'FILL' 1, 'wght' 400, 'GRAD' 0, 'opsz' 24; }
```

In `@theme` we can hook the variable font's default variation settings to a token:

```css
@theme {
  --font-material-symbols: "Material Symbols Outlined";
  --font-material-symbols--font-variation-settings: "FILL" 0, "wght" 400, "GRAD" 0, "opsz" 24;
}
```

Then a one-line utility for the filled variant:

```css
@utility material-symbols-filled {
  font-variation-settings: "FILL" 1, "wght" 400, "GRAD" 0, "opsz" 24;
}
```

## Nav Component Inventory

Reference pattern: Stitch HTML lines 177-211.

| Subcomponent | Render | Client needed? | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `<nav>` outer shell | Server | No | `fixed top-0 w-full z-50 bg-surface-container-lowest/80 backdrop-blur-md shadow-sm transition-all duration-300` |
| Inner container | Server | No | `flex justify-between items-center max-w-[1200px] mx-auto px-margin-mobile md:px-gutter h-20` |
| Brand link | Server | No | Material Symbol `apartment` (filled) + "Axil" text. Uses `font-headline-lg text-headline-lg font-bold text-primary flex items-center gap-2` |
| Desktop nav links | Server | No | 4 anchor tags: `text-on-surface-variant hover:text-primary transition-colors font-medium` |
| CTA button | Server | No | `<a>` (not `<button>` — it scrolls to `#contacto`, not a real submit). `bg-primary text-on-primary px-6 py-2.5 rounded-lg hover:opacity-90 transition-opacity font-medium shadow-sm hover:shadow-md` |
| Mobile menu trigger | **Client** | **Yes** | `useState` for open/closed + `onClick` |
| Mobile menu container | Server | No | Rendered as `<div id="mobile-menu" class="md:hidden bg-surface-container-lowest border-t border-surface-variant hidden">` — visibility toggled by client adding/removing `hidden` |
| Scroll-shadow effect | **Client** | **Yes** | `useEffect` listening to `window.scrollY > 20`, toggles `shadow-md` on the nav |

**Recommended split**:

- `app/_components/nav/nav.tsx` — Server Component. Renders the full nav HTML, accepts no props in this change.
- `app/_components/nav/mobile-menu-toggle.tsx` — Client Component. Owns both the mobile menu state AND the scroll-shadow effect (both are trivial, both touch the same DOM node). Exposes a render-prop or wraps the trigger button + injects a ref to the mobile menu container.

A simpler alternative is to have the client component own the entire `nav` element (including the static parts) and just split off the static brand/text from the interactive bits. The recommendation above is preferred because Server Components are smaller bundles, and the static parts are pure data.

**Nav links content** (Spanish, from Stitch HTML): `Problema`, `Soluciones`, `Producto`, `FAQ` — these are placeholders for the real sections. The proposal should confirm with the user whether the Nav links should match the Stitch HTML exactly (anchors to `#problema`, `#soluciones`, `#producto`, `#faq`) or be updated to match the actual follow-up changes.

**CTA label** (Spanish): "Demo" in the desktop nav, "Solicitar Demo" in the mobile menu. Both anchor to `#contacto`.

## Next.js 15 + Tailwind 4 + pnpm Setup Gotchas

These are non-obvious steps the proposal must call out (verified via Next.js and Tailwind v4 official docs):

1. **Scaffold command**:
   ```bash
   pnpm create next-app@latest . --ts --eslint --tailwind --app --src-dir=false --import-alias "@/*" --turbopack
   ```
   Or with `--yes` for full defaults. **Note**: `create-next-app` will refuse to install into a non-empty directory unless you pass the directory as `.` AND there is no `package.json` (we're clear). It will, however, warn about the existing `DESIGN.md` and `openspec/` files — confirm in the proposal that this is OK.

2. **Tailwind 4 PostCSS plugin name changed**: in v3 it was `tailwindcss`. In v4 it is `@tailwindcss/postcss` and must be installed as a dev dependency:
   ```bash
   pnpm add -D tailwindcss @tailwindcss/postcss
   ```
   `postcss.config.mjs` becomes:
   ```js
   export default { plugins: { "@tailwindcss/postcss": {} } };
   ```

3. **No `tailwind.config.js` is needed** (or recommended) in Tailwind 4. All theme configuration goes in `app/globals.css` via the `@theme` block. Do **not** create one.

4. **The CSS entry file uses `@import "tailwindcss"`** — not the v3 `@tailwind base/components/utilities` triple. This goes at the top of `app/globals.css`.

5. **Theme namespace syntax**: `--color-primary`, `--text-headline-xl`, `--spacing-stack-md`, `--radius-xl`, `--shadow-ambient`, `--font-hanken`, `--container-max`, `--font-weight-*`, `--tracking-*`, `--leading-*`. Names **must** start with one of Tailwind's known namespaces to generate utilities; arbitrary names only become CSS variables.

6. **Variable font `axes` for Material Symbols Outlined**: must be passed explicitly or variation settings will not work. Document this in the proposal because it's easy to miss.

7. **`<html lang>` is `es`** (Spanish) per the Stitch HTML and the product's market. The `viewport` and `metadata` in `app/layout.tsx` should match.

8. **Spanish metadata**: title `"Axil - Digitalización de Alquileres en República Dominicana"` (from Stitch HTML `<title>`) and a Spanish description. The proposal should confirm these or ask the user.

9. **Vercel deploy**: zero-config, but if we want edge runtime for the layout (unnecessary for a static page), declare it in `next.config.ts`. Not needed for this change.

10. **`next/font` variables on `<html>`**: the three font variables (`--font-hanken-grotesk`, `--font-inter`, `--font-material-symbols`) must be applied to the root `<html>` element via `className`, not in CSS, because the variables are populated by `next/font` at build time.

## Approaches Considered

### 1. Color palette: full `@theme` vs. lazy subset

| Approach | Pros | Cons | Effort |
|---|---|---|---|
| **A. Full 49-color `@theme` (recommended)** | Establishes the design system in one pass; `bg-primary-container/20` and similar future utilities work without re-mapping; matches Stitch's exhaustive `tailwind.config` | Larger CSS output (still < 5KB after purge) | Low |
| B. Only the 6 tokens the Nav uses | Smaller initial CSS, simpler to read | Forces a re-map every time a new section is added; breaks "design system as source of truth"; user has to touch CSS for every new component | Low |

### 2. Typography: semantic tokens vs. utility composition

| Approach | Pros | Cons | Effort |
|---|---|---|---|
| **A. `--text-headline-xl` paired with line-height/weight/tracking (recommended)** | One class `<h1 className="text-headline-xl text-on-surface">` per headline; matches DESIGN.md naming | Slightly more verbose CSS to write | Low |
| B. Compose `text-[48px] leading-[56px] font-bold tracking-[-0.02em]` per element | No upfront theme work; escape hatch | Loses semantic meaning; hard to keep consistent; violates tailwind-4 skill rule against arbitrary values for design-system properties | Low |

### 3. Nav interactivity: single client component vs. two

| Approach | Pros | Cons | Effort |
|---|---|---|---|
| **A. One client component for menu + scroll (recommended)** | Less wiring; both effects touch the same `<nav>` element; React 19's compiler will handle optimization | Slightly larger client bundle (but trivially so) | Low |
| B. Two client components, one per effect | Each component is single-purpose; easier to test in isolation | Overhead of two `"use client"` boundaries; needs prop drilling or context to share the nav ref | Low |
| C. Pure CSS with `:has()` and `details/summary` | Zero JS; progressive enhancement | Cannot do scroll-shadow without JS; `details/summary` doesn't match Stitch's open/close + rotate-45 icon | Medium |

### 4. Page placeholder

| Approach | Pros | Cons | Effort |
|---|---|---|---|
| A. `<main className="..." />` empty | Minimal; hero change is the obvious next step | Doesn't communicate "coming soon" to anyone viewing the dev server | Low |
| **B. Empty `<main>` + a centered "Coming soon" notice (recommended)** | Clear signal that this is a foundation; useful for visual review of layout/nav without distractions | Has to be removed in the next change | Low |
| C. Sectioned comment stubs (one `<section>` per upcoming change) | Pre-wires layout; future changes just fill in | Hard to keep up to date; mixes placeholder and real sections | Medium |

## Recommendation

1. **Run the scaffold first** (`pnpm create next-app@latest . --ts --eslint --tailwind --app --import-alias "@/*" --turbopack --yes`) before any manual edits, so the canonical Next.js 15 + Tailwind 4 baseline is in place. Treat the scaffold output as **read-only** for the first commit.
2. **Map the entire 49-color palette** to `@theme` colors in `app/globals.css`. Establish the design system once.
3. **Map the 6 typography tokens** using Tailwind v4's paired suffix syntax (`--text-headline-xl--line-height`, etc.).
4. **Add `--font-material-symbols`** to `@theme` so Material Symbols inherits the variable font's default variation settings automatically.
5. **Use `next/font/google`** for Hanken Grotesk, Inter, and Material Symbols Outlined. The Material Symbols call **must** include `axes: ['FILL', 'wght', 'GRAD', 'opsz']`.
6. **Nav as Server Component shell + one Client Component** for the mobile menu trigger and scroll effect. Keep the brand, links, and CTA static.
7. **Page placeholder: a centered "Coming soon" notice** inside `<main>` so the next change has a visible diff and the dev server is useful.
8. **Spanish `<html lang="es">`**, Spanish metadata title + description, taken from the Stitch HTML unless the user says otherwise.

## Risks

### CRITICAL

- **Tailwind v4 setup is not covered by any installed skill.** The `tailwind-4` skill (v1.1) covers utility-class usage patterns and the `cn()` helper, but does NOT cover the `@import "tailwindcss"` directive, the `@theme` block, the `--color-*` namespace convention, the PostCSS plugin name change (`@tailwindcss/postcss`), or the `next/font` integration. The proposal must rely on the official Tailwind v4 docs (already consulted for this exploration) to write the `globals.css` correctly. If anyone re-applies a v3-style `tailwind.config.js`, the build will silently produce no utilities.

### WARNING

- **Card radius mismatch between DESIGN.md and Stitch HTML.** DESIGN.md says cards use 1.5rem (`rounded-xl`). The actual Stitch HTML uses `rounded-2xl` (1rem) for "Why Axil?" cards and `rounded-lg` (0.5rem) for pain-point cards. The proposal must ask the user which radius is the "default card" for the upcoming section changes, otherwise the design system will have inconsistent radii. The `@theme` should expose all three so both are available, but the proposal should make a recommendation and ask the user to confirm.
- **Spanish copy is not confirmed by the user.** The Stitch HTML is in Spanish, but the user has been writing to me in English. The Nav links (`Problema`, `Soluciones`, `Producto`, `FAQ`), CTA (`Demo`, `Solicitar Demo`), metadata title, and `<html lang>` are all Spanish. The proposal should ask: keep Spanish, or translate to English?
- **No ESLint or formatter convention exists in the project.** `create-next-app --yes` will install ESLint with Next's flat config (no Prettier, no Husky). The proposal should either accept the scaffold default or add Prettier/Husky as a separate concern. Recommend: accept the default, defer Prettier+Husky to a future "dev-tooling" change.
- **The change is on a branch that doesn't exist yet** (only `main` with the init commit). The orchestrator should verify whether the user wants a feature branch (`feat/landing-foundation`) or wants to commit directly to `main`. Convention from the repo's single commit doesn't establish a pattern.
- **Vercel deploy target means strict Node version.** The proposal should pin Node (e.g. `"engines": { "node": ">=20" }` in `package.json`) and let `pnpm` resolve otherwise.

### SUGGESTION

- **Consider adding a `predev` / `prebuild` script** that runs `pnpm lint` and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`, so the dev server doesn't start with type or lint errors. Standard Next.js setup, easy to add.
- **Add `.nvmrc`** with the Node version for Vercel reproducibility.
- **Consider enabling React Compiler** in the scaffold (`create-next-app` now asks for this). React 19 + Compiler removes the need for `useMemo` / `useCallback`. The `react-19` skill is consistent with this.
- **Material Symbols Outlined weight is variable**; the Stitch HTML uses `text-[28px]` and `text-[32px]` for different sizes. This works as long as `opsz` is in the loaded `axes`. Verified.
- **The `font-variation-settings` for Material Symbols** are duplicated in the Stitch HTML (`<link href="...Material+Symbols+Outlined..." />` appears twice). When we use `next/font`, we deduplicate this automatically.
- **The `scroll-smooth` class** on `<html>` in the Stitch HTML is not in DESIGN.md. Recommend including it in `app/layout.tsx` because anchor-based nav (`href="#problema"`) is core to the design.

## Ready for Proposal

**Yes** — with three open questions for the user that the proposal phase should surface:

1. **Card radius default**: 1.5rem (DESIGN.md) vs. 1rem (Stitch HTML "Why Axil?" cards) vs. 0.5rem (Stitch HTML pain-point cards)?
2. **Language**: keep Spanish (matching the Stitch HTML) or translate to English for the scaffold?
3. **Nav link labels**: keep the Stitch placeholder text (`Problema`, `Soluciones`, `Producto`, `FAQ`, `Demo`) or wait for the upcoming section changes to finalize the real labels?

If the user defers all three (e.g. "use your best judgment"), the recommendation is: **1.5rem as the default card radius (matches DESIGN.md intent), Spanish (matches the market + Stitch), placeholder Nav labels with a comment noting they will be re-confirmed when sections ship.**
